title: Navigate From the Hospital HMI Main Menu to Available Menus
summary: Use the Hospital HMI Main Menu and its documented right-side function keys to open available menus for Hospital Station tote handling. The source identifies access to Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms from the Main Menu, and documents quick-access mappings for F1 through F6.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to navigate on the Hospital HMI from the Main Menu to one of the documented menus using the on-screen menu options or the right-side quick-access function keys.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for performing actions inside the Maintenance Menu; the source only documents navigation to that menu, not maintenance procedures.
Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented menu behavior, permissions, or actions beyond the Main Menu access and function-key mappings shown in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Hospital HMI Main Menu and use the documented menu selections or right-side function keys to open the desired menu.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to HMI navigation only.
Review role appropriateness before using maintenance functions, because the source does not describe what actions are permitted inside the Maintenance Menu.
access_or_tools_needed: Documented F-key control mapping from Table 4-12

system_preconditions: Hospital Station Main Menu is available on the HMI.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI Main Menu screen showing access points and right-side quick-access buttons.
Hospital HMI Operations screen as an example destination screen reached from menu navigation.

steps.title: Open or view the Hospital HMI Main Menu
steps.purpose: Confirm the operator is on the documented Hospital Station Main Menu before using menu navigation controls.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Hospital Station Main Menu on the HMI.
steps.expected_result: The Hospital HMI Main Menu is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed screen matches the documented Hospital HMI Main Menu layout.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed screen does not match the documented Main Menu or the operator cannot access the Main Menu.

actions.label: View Main Menu
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Hospital Station Main Menu on the HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI Main Menu screen layout and right-side quick-access buttons.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The hospital station Main Menu allows access to three other menus: Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, Alarms.
The hospital HMI screens are used to handle totes that have been directed to the Hospital Station.

steps.title: Identify the available destination menus
steps.purpose: Verify which menus are documented as available from the Hospital HMI Main Menu.
steps.instruction: Identify the available destination menus listed from the Main Menu: Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms.
steps.expected_result: The operator recognizes the documented destination menus available from the Main Menu.
steps.healthy_condition: Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms are identifiable from the Main Menu documentation or display.
steps.failure_condition: The available menus shown do not match the documented Main Menu options.

actions.label: Identify menu options
actions.exact_action: Identify the available destination menus listed from the Main Menu: Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Menu access points for Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The hospital station Main Menu allows access to three other menus: Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, Alarms.

steps.title: Use quick-access buttons to open the desired menu
steps.purpose: Navigate directly from the Main Menu to the desired documented menu using the right-side function keys.
steps.instruction: Use the right-hand quick-access buttons as documented to navigate: press F1 for Operator menu, F2 for Maintenance menu, or F6 for Alarms.
steps.expected_result: The selected menu opens from the Main Menu.
steps.healthy_condition: The chosen destination menu opens using the documented F-key mapping.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing the documented function key does not open the expected menu.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side quick-access buttons and their menu assignments.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.
The selected function key does not open the expected menu.
Maintenance functions are needed beyond navigation and role appropriateness is unclear.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: F5 Main menu; F3 Previous screen; F1 Operator menu; F2 Maintenance menu; F4 Setup menu; F6 Alarms.
The six buttons on the right-hand side of the screen offer quick access to menus.

steps.title: Use Main menu or Previous screen navigation if needed
steps.purpose: Return to the Main Menu or move back one screen using the documented navigation keys.
steps.instruction: If needed for navigation, use F5 for Main menu or F3 for Previous screen as documented.
steps.expected_result: The HMI returns to the Main Menu or the previous screen as selected.
steps.healthy_condition: F5 returns to Main menu and F3 returns to the previous screen as documented.
steps.failure_condition: The navigation keys do not return the expected screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Main Menu screen and right-side quick-access controls including F5 and F3.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.
F5 or F3 does not navigate to the expected screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: F5 Main menu; F3 Previous screen; F1 Operator menu; F2 Maintenance menu; F4 Setup menu; F6 Alarms.
Figure 4-29 appears on page 59 in section 4.1.3.1 Main Menu.

steps.title: Use Setup menu quick access if needed
steps.purpose: Access the Setup menu using the documented quick-access mapping when Setup navigation is needed.
steps.instruction: If Setup menu access is needed from the quick-access controls, use F4 as listed in Table 4-12.
steps.expected_result: The Setup menu is opened using the documented quick-access key.
steps.healthy_condition: F4 opens the Setup menu as listed in the source.
steps.failure_condition: F4 does not open the Setup menu or the mapping shown on the HMI differs from the documented mapping.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side quick-access controls including the F4 Setup menu mapping.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.
F4 does not open the Setup menu as listed in Table 4-12.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: F5 Main menu; F3 Previous screen; F1 Operator menu; F2 Maintenance menu; F4 Setup menu; F6 Alarms.

success_criteria: The Hospital HMI Main Menu is displayed and matches the documented layout.
The operator can identify the documented available menus from the Main Menu.
The selected menu opens using the documented quick-access function key mapping.
healthy_conditions: The Main Menu shows access to Operator Menu, Maintenance Menu, and Alarms.
The right-side quick-access buttons match the documented mappings: F1 Operator menu, F2 Maintenance menu, F3 Previous screen, F4 Setup menu, F5 Main menu, F6 Alarms.
Navigation keys open the expected destination screens.
failure_conditions: The displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.
A documented function key does not open the expected menu.
The operator needs maintenance actions beyond navigation, but the source does not define those actions or role boundaries.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed screen does not show the documented Main Menu options or F-key mappings.
Escalate if a documented function key does not open the expected menu.
Review role appropriateness before using maintenance functions, because the source does not describe what actions are permitted inside the Maintenance Menu.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not define role boundaries for access to Maintenance Menu or Setup menu.
The source does not describe actions to perform inside the Maintenance Menu, Setup menu, or Alarms after navigation.
The source section text is not provided in the packet beyond referenced summaries and artifact retrieval text.
